description_long: This object consists of two large, rectangular solar panel arrays mounted on a flat, light gray rooftop structure. The solar panels are composed of dark, grid-patterned photovoltaic cells, each array supported by angled mounting frames that elevate them above the roof surface. The panels are positioned at a noticeable tilt, likely to optimize sunlight capture, and are separated by a central rooftop section. The surrounding rooftop features low parapet walls and a few raised architectural elements, suggesting an urban building context. The overall structure appears robust, with the solar panels dominating the visual impression. No visible cabling or inverter equipment is apparent from any angle.
description: A pair of tilted, grid-patterned solar panel arrays mounted on a flat urban rooftop with low parapet walls and supporting frames.
description_view_0: Front view showing both solar panel arrays symmetrically mounted on the rooftop, with their grid patterns clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the tilt angle of the solar panels and the elevation provided by their mounting frames.
description_view_2: Rear view where the solar panels are partially obscured by rooftop structures, with only the upper edges visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the tilt and separation of the two solar panel arrays, as well as more rooftop surface details.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, facing away from the central rooftop section, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: roof-mounted with angled aluminum or steel frames on a flat building roof
attribute_array_configuration: two separate arrays, each consisting of multiple panels arranged in a rectangular grid, positioned parallel but separated by a central rooftop section
